title: Use read_file to read example.py and list function names

messages_history.content: Use read_file to read example.py and list function names

The functions defined in `example.py` are as follows:

1. `greet(name: str) -> str`
2. `add(a: int, b: int) -> int`
3. `multiply(x: int, y: int) -> int`
4. `Calculator.add(self, x: float) -> float`
5. `Calculator.subtract(self, x: float) -> float`
6. `Calculator.multiply(self, x: float) -> float`
7. `Calculator.divide(self, x: float) -> Optional[float]`
8. `Calculator.reset(self) -> None`
9. `Calculator.get_history(self) -> list`
10. `StringUtils.reverse(s: str) -> str`
11. `StringUtils.is_palindrome(s: str) -> bool`
12. `StringUtils.count_words(s: str) -> int`

If you need further assistance or details about any specific function, feel free to ask!
